_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to identify tools and techniques available for analyzing binary applications and interpreted scripts.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, including file system structure, process management, user and permission models, package management, and common command-line utilities for text processing and system monitoring.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-008 (TF, cosine: 0.485), TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.456).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.3/10 (raw 0.334). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [ability, analyzing, applications, available, binary, identify] vs WIDAI-only terms [command, common, data, engineering, file, fundamentals] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.870 (P=0.882, R=0.858). P exceeds R by 0.023 — the DCWF element's 14 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 32 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.4%). "Ability to identify tools and techniques available for analyzing binary applicat" and "Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, i" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.490 > STS 0.334 (gap +0.156). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.9%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [ability, analyzing, applications, available, binary, identify]. WIDAI terms: [command, common, data, engineering, file, fundamentals].
